steps.title: Identify corresponding angles from similarity statement
steps.content: The statement △CAE ~ △CBD specifies the vertex correspondence: C↔C, A↔B, E↔D. In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal, so the angles at the corresponding vertices are equal: ∠CAE = ∠CBD.

steps.title: Apply the given angle measure
steps.content: We're given that ∠CBD = 56°. Since ∠CAE = ∠CBD from the correspondence, ∠CAE = 56°. That matches choice B — B is correct.
